article_title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Under MIT License, Putting a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Open Model Within Three to Six Months of the Frontier
summary: Two editorial errors require correction: uncited Al Jazeera URL in body and Huawei Ascend claim misattributed to TechCrunch

findings.message: Al Jazeera URL cited inline but absent from sources array
findings.details: The body links to https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2026/4/24/chinas-deepseek-unveils-latest-model-a-year-after-upending-global-tech for the government bans claim, but this URL does not appear in the submission's sources array. Additionally, the article says 'the US...had barred government use' but Al Jazeera reports 'multiple US states' (not the federal US government). Add the Al Jazeera URL to sources and correct 'the US' to 'multiple US states'.

findings.message: Huawei Ascend compatibility claim wrongly attributed to TechCrunch
findings.details: The article states 'As TechCrunch reports, V4 is compatible with Huawei's Ascend chips, with Huawei announcing full platform support for the new models.' However, WebFetch of the TechCrunch article confirms it contains no mention of Huawei Ascend chip compatibility. The Huawei Ascend 950 production scaling is supported by Fortune, but the specific 'full platform support' and V4-Huawei compatibility attribution to TechCrunch is a misattribution. Fix by re-attributing to a source that actually makes this claim, or remove the claim if unverifiable.

content_preview.title: DeepSeek Releases V4 Under MIT License, Putting a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Open Model Within Three to Six Months of the Frontier
content_preview.summary: DeepSeek's V4-Pro and V4-Flash arrive with 1M-token context, three reasoning modes, and pricing that undercuts frontier rivals by up to 8x.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released preview versions of two new large language models on April 24, 2026 — DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash — both published under the MIT License. The release marks the lab's first major model launch since its R1 reasoning model rattled Silicon Valley in early 2025, and it arrives with a familiar combination: competitive benchmark performance, dramatically lower pricing than Western rivals, and fully open weights available on Hugging Face.

Accordin...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (1007 words, within 400–1200 range). Good section organization covering specs, benchmarks, pricing, geopolitical context, and analysis. Two editorial errors require correction before approval.
editor_notes.source_verification: DeepSeek API docs: fetched — V4-Pro 1.6T/49B active ✓, V4-Flash 284B/13B ✓, 1M context ✓, MIT license NOT explicitly confirmed in text but implied; Huawei compatibility NOT mentioned. TechCrunch: fetched — 1.6T/49B confirmed ✓, '3 to 6 months behind frontier' ✓, NO HUAWEI ASCEND MENTION ✗ (article claims 'As TechCrunch reports, V4 is compatible with Huawei's Ascend chips' — this is a misattribution). Fortune: fetched — V4-Pro pricing $3.48/M output ✓, V4-Flash $0.28/M output ✓, Huawei Ascend 950 production scaling ✓. Silicon Republic: fetched — Tencent $40B valuation, 20% stake ✓. Hugging Face: fetched — all benchmark scores confirmed: LiveCodeBench 93.5 ✓, Codeforces 3206 ✓, SWE-bench 80.6% ✓, HMMT 95.2 ✓, IMOAnswerBench 89.8 ✓, MRCR 83.5 ✓, MIT license ✓, Think Max ≥384K tokens ✓. Simon Willison: fetched — 27% FLOPs, 10% KV cache ✓, 865 GB V4-Pro / 160 GB V4-Flash ✓. DataCamp: HTTP 403 to automated checker but fetched manually — pricing table GPT-5.5 $30/M output ✓, Opus 4.7 $25/M output ✓, MMLU-Pro 87.5% ✓, GPQA Diamond 90.1% ✓. Al Jazeera: NOT in sources array but cited in body — fetched and confirmed: Australia, Taiwan, South Korea, Denmark, Italy bans confirmed ✓; however Al Jazeera says 'multiple US states' not 'the US' ✗.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Most claims verified. Two errors: (1) Al Jazeera URL cited in body for government bans claim but absent from sources array; the article also overstates 'the US' when Al Jazeera says 'multiple US states'. (2) Huawei Ascend compatibility claim attributed to TechCrunch but TechCrunch article does not mention Huawei Ascend at all — this is a clear misattribution. The Huawei Ascend 950 production scaling is attributed to Fortune (correct), but 'V4 is compatible with Huawei's Ascend chips, with Huawei announcing full platform support' is attributed to TechCrunch (incorrect). All other facts verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Analysis section appropriately hedges benchmark claims as self-reported. Geopolitical context handled factually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald article covers DeepSeek V4. Distinct topic from any recent DeepSeek coverage.
concerns: Al Jazeera URL cited inline (in body hyperlink) but absent from sources array — editorial policy requires all cited sources to appear in sources array
Huawei Ascend compatibility claim wrongly attributed to TechCrunch — TechCrunch article contains zero mention of Huawei Ascend chips
recommendations: Change 'the US...had barred government use' to 'multiple US states...had introduced restrictions' to match what Al Jazeera actually reports

Re-attribute the Huawei Ascend compatibility claim to a source that actually says it (e.g., a DeepSeek announcement or separate tech outlet), or remove 'Huawei announcing full platform support' if unverifiable
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong article with good coverage of a major AI release. Two source attribution errors must be fixed: add the Al Jazeera URL to sources and correct the TechCrunch Huawei misattribution. Ready for approval once corrected.
